audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel / Shemuel Bet chapter 5, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for tribal coronation, shepherd/ruler language, reign chronology, capture of Jebusite Jerusalem, blind/lame taunt and textual difficulty, royal construction, Hiram's labor, expanding wives and concubines, dynastic children, Philistine campaigns, Divine-war language, abandoned sacred images, and territorial pursuit.

verses.restored: All tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron and said, Look, we are your bone and your flesh.

Also yesterday and before, when Saul was king over us, you were the one bringing Israel out and bringing it in. YHWH said to you, You shall shepherd my people Israel, and you shall be leader over Israel.
All elders of Israel came to king at Hebron, and king David cut covenant with them at Hebron before YHWH. They anointed David as king over Israel.
David was thirty years old when he became king; forty years he reigned.
In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years over all Israel and Judah.

King and his men went to Jerusalem against Jebusites, inhabitants of land. They said to David, You shall not come in here, because even blind and lame will turn you away, saying, David cannot come in here.
David captured stronghold of Zion; it is City of David.
David said on that day, Whoever strikes Jebusites, let him reach through water shaft—and the lame and blind, hated by David's soul. Therefore they say, Blind and lame shall not come into house.
David lived in stronghold and called it City of David. David built all around from Millo and inward.
David went on and became great, and YHWH, Divine of hosts, was with him.
Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, cedar trees, woodworkers, and stoneworkers for walls, and they built a house for David.
David knew that YHWH had established him as king over Israel and had lifted up his kingdom for sake of YHWH's people Israel.
David took more concubines and wives from Jerusalem after he came from Hebron, and more sons and daughters were born to David.
These are names of those born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
Ibhar, Elishua, Nepheg, Japhia,
Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet.
Philistines heard that they had anointed David as king over Israel, and all Philistines went up to seek David. David heard and went down to stronghold.
Philistines came and spread themselves in valley of Rephaim.
David inquired of YHWH, saying, Shall I go up against Philistines? Will you give them into my hand? YHWH said to David, Go up, because giving I will give Philistines into your hand.
David came to Baal-perazim, and David struck them there. He said, YHWH has broken through my enemies before me like breach of waters. Therefore he called name of that place Baal-perazim.
They abandoned their sacred images there, and David and his men carried them away.
Philistines again went up and spread themselves in valley of Rephaim.
David inquired of YHWH, and YHWH said, You shall not go up directly. Circle around behind them and come against them opposite balsam trees.
When you hear sound of marching in tops of balsam trees, then act decisively, because then YHWH has gone out before you to strike camp of Philistines.
David did so as YHWH commanded him, and he struck Philistines from Geba as far as approach to Gezer.

translation_notes: Israel's elders use kinship, military memory, covenant, and Divine speech to legitimate David. None of these removes the people's right to hold a ruler accountable.
The capture of Jerusalem is conquest of an inhabited Jebusite city, not discovery of empty sacred space. Jebusites must not disappear beneath later Davidic or Zion traditions.

Verses 6 and 8 contain a deeply ableist taunt, response, and proverb, with significant textual and interpretive difficulty. Blindness and mobility disability are not contemptible, impure, or evidence of exclusion from sacred community.
The phrase translated 'hated by David's soul' may preserve hostile propaganda or a damaged tradition. It cannot be used to authorize discrimination against disabled people.

Hiram's cedar, craftspeople, and construction represent international diplomacy and labor. Royal architecture is not proof of Divine approval and should be examined for extraction and worker conditions.
David's additional wives and concubines expand dynasty and alliances. Their names, voices, and consent are absent; they are not reproductive infrastructure for empire.
Philistines remain human communities, not timeless enemies. Their sacred images may not be used to justify religious contempt, vandalism, or modern ethnic hatred.
Statements that YHWH commands or grants victory belong to ancient royal war theology and must never authorize modern conquest, nationalism, or claims of guaranteed military favor.
The chapter's growth language should be read beside the people and bodies absorbed by expanding royal power.
safety_note: Maximum anti-ableist and anti-imperial safeguards: Do not repeat the blind/lame language as a spiritual insult or exclusion rule. Do not romanticize Jerusalem's capture, dynastic marriage expansion, sacred-image seizure, or territorial war. Anointing and victory never create leader immunity or modern land entitlement.
